items.title: Salah happy to stay at Liverpool - Thursday's gossip
items.summary: Mohamed Salah would be happy to stay at Liverpool until 2027, there are six Premier League clubs interested in Frankfurt's Nnamdi Collins, while Casemiro wants to keep playing in Europe.

items.title: Galatasaray score twice in extra time to squash 10-man Juventus
items.summary: Galatasaray survived a 10-man Juventus comeback, scoring twice in extra time to win 7-5 on aggregate, despite Lloyd Kelly's red card.

items.title: Vinícius seals Madrid progress amid Benfica boos
items.summary: Benfica fans loudly booed Real Madrid forward Vinícius Junior at the Santiago Bernabéu Stadium in the second leg of the Champions League playoffs on Wednesday.

items.title: VAR hands Atalanta winning pen after bloody foul
items.summary: Two red cards, a VAR overturn and a decisive converted penalty with the last kick of the game that came after a bloody foul on Atalanta's Nikola Krstovic.

items.title: PSG advance to set up Barça or Chelsea clash
items.summary: Mamadou Coulibaly's red card cost his side dearly as AS Monaco drew 2-2 at defending champion Paris Saint-Germain in the second leg of their Champions League playoff on Wednesday and went out 5-4 on aggregate.

items.title: Bouanga signs multiyear DP extension with LAFC
items.summary: High-scoring forward Denis Bouanga has agreed to a multiyear contract extension with LAFC.

items.title: Championship roundup: Coventry battle back to see off Sheffield United
items.summary: Haji Wright and Jack Rudoni seal 2-1 comeback win Millwall beat Birmingham to keep up promotion push Coventry extended their lead at the top of the Championship to five points after coming from behind to win 2-1 at Sheffield United, while Millwall went third by beating Birmingham City 3-0 on Wednesday. After a run of two wins in eight games caused Frank Lampard’s men to blow a comfortable advantage in the race for promotion to the Premier League, Coventry have bounced back with three consecutive wins. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Darlings of Italian football' - how Atalanta salvaged national pride
items.summary: As Italy faced the embarrassment of having no clubs in the Champions League last 16, Atalanta became the saviours of national reputation - and pride.

items.title: Champions League roundup: Atalanta oust Dortmund, Galatasaray thwart Juventus fightback
items.summary: Serie A side win 4-3 on aggregate after 4-1 victory Istanbul giants fend off 10-man Juve in extra time Lazar Samardzic slotted home a stoppage-time penalty to complete a dramatic 4-1 victory for Atalanta against Borussia Dortmund on Wednesday, sending the Italian side into the Champions League’s last 16 with a comeback 4-3 aggregate triumph. Dortmund’s Ramy Bensebaini was sent off after his studs caught the head of Atalanta’s Nikola Krstovic in the penalty area and Samardzic converted the spot kick in the 98th minute to send the Italians through. Atalanta will now face either Arsenal or Bayern Munich in the round of 16, with the draw on Friday. Continue reading...

items.title: Courtois is blij met dansje van Vinícius en vreest voor Manchester City
items.summary: Het dansje van Vinícius Júnior van woensdag ontging ploeggenoot Thibaut Courtois, maar het doet de keeper van Real Madrid deugd dat zijn ploegmakker danst, na alle ophef van de afgelopen week. Real bekerde tegen Benfica door in de Champions League. Courtois vreest dat Real in de volgende ronde weer Manchester City zal loten.

items.title: Achtste finales Champions League: alle clubs en mogelijke affiches
items.summary: De tussenronde van de Champions League zit erop en dat betekent dat we zijn aangekomen in de achtste finales van het toernooi. Daarin zijn nog zestien clubs vertegenwoordigd. Voetbal International schetst welke clubs dit zijn én wat de affiches in de volgende ronde kunnen worden.

items.title: Real Madrid verlengt KO-streak van geschorste Mourinho
items.summary: Woensdagavond werden de laatste returns in de tussenronde van de Champions League gepeeld. Real Madrid en Paris Saint-Germain hoopten het klusje te klaren. Atalanta en Juventus hadden een comeback nodig. Lees de volledige avond terug in de VI Live.

items.title: Wanneer is loting achtste finale Champions League en waar wordt die uitgezonden?
items.summary: De tussenronde van de Champions League zit erop en dus kunnen we vooruitkijken richting de loting voor de achtste finale. Zestien ploegen doen daaraan mee. In dit artikel alles over de Champions League-loting.

items.title: Tiental Juve koopt niets voor ongekende comeback: Lang en Galatasaray door in CL
items.summary: Galatasaray heeft woensdagavond de achtste finale van de Champions League bereikt. Dat ging niet zonder slag of stoot. De Turken gaven een marge van drie goals weg tegen een Turijns tiental, maar stelden in de verlenging orde op zaken. Zo maakt basisklant Noa Lang zich op voor een ontmoeting met Liverpool of Tottenham Hotspur.

items.title: Vinícius has last word as Real Madrid wrap up victory over Benfica
items.summary: Vinícius Júnior ran to the corner and danced again, just as he had done in Lisbon a week ago, but this time there was celebration all around him. There was also relief. With eleven minutes left on a sometimes nervous night at the Santiago Bernabéu, he had been set free to run through, putting the ball past Anatoliy Trubin and Real Madrid into the last 16 of the Champions League. A 3-1 aggregate lead secured, a place in the next round too, he set off and shook his hips in front of the flag, fans released from their fears and applauding him as he did so. One-nil up from a first leg in Lisbon that had been overshadowed by allegations that the Brazilian had been racially abused by Benfica’s Gianluca Prestianni, Madrid had trailed by an early goal here and although they equalised swiftly, an uneasy night ensued in which they had not felt safe until then. In the end though, with Prestianni and José Mourinho absent and nowhere to be seen, an impressive performance from Benfica was not enough. Continue reading...

items.title: 'He was always going to score' - Real beat Benfica to reach last 16
items.summary: Watch as Real Madrid head through to the last 16 in the Champions League after a 2-1 victory against Benfica.

items.title: Off the Court: Netball Super Cup reflections and ranking England players
Champions League as it happened: Gala's extra-time win over Juve caps chaotic day
items.summary: Galatasaray stunned Juventus and alongside Paris Saint-Germain and Real Madrid, are off to the round of 16 on after a wild day of Champions League action.

items.title: Draper's tour comeback ended by Rinderknech in Dubai
items.summary: Jack Draper's return to ATP Tour action came to an end on Wednesday as he was beaten by Arthur Rinderknech in a gruelling three-set encounter at the Dubai Tennis Championships.

items.title: Martin O’Neill claims VAR ‘debilitating’ for officials after Celtic fail with red card appeal
items.summary: Auston Trusty sent off against Hibernian O’Neill: ‘In time we won’t need a referee’ Celtic’s manager, Martin O’Neill, has delivered a withering assessment of VAR in Scottish football after what he called the “ridiculous” dismissal of Auston Trusty during Sunday’s loss by Hibernian. Trusty’s red card and a failed Celtic appeal mean the centre-back will miss three crucial Scottish Premiership games, starting with Sunday’s Old Firm visit to Ibrox. O’Neill claimed VAR actions must be “debilitating” for on-field officials. Continue reading...
